experiment.name: Synthetic data and physics identifiability qualification
experiment.goal: Determine which supplied and newly controlled synthetic variables can support valid-versus-misspecified physics experiments before neural training.
experiment.hypothesis: The supplied v2 dataset supports leakage-safe RUL evaluation but not known-truth physics applicability, while the official MATLAB simulator can produce a labeled controlled benchmark with recorded progression law and hidden degradation state.

success_criteria: The supplied Data.mat fingerprint and 28/12 official partition are verified.
Every supplied trajectory is exported without modifying raw data and ends at normalized RUL zero.
The intentional absence of supplied degradation-family and fault labels is recorded as an identifiability limitation.
MATLAB version and required toolbox availability are recorded.
The official simulator completes a deterministic labeled smoke run and exposes the progression family, hidden degradation trajectory, and fault location.
The 40-scenario controlled design and immutable unit-level splits pass validation.
No neural-network training or test-label-driven model selection occurs.
